recently.definition: Not long ago; a short time before now.
recently.example: I recently started learning to cook.

routine.definition: A performance, usually comedy, done in front of an audience.
routine.example: The comedian did a funny routine about families.

comedian.definition: A person whose job is to make people laugh.
comedian.example: The comedian told jokes about daily life.

fantasy.definition: An imagined situation that is not real, usually something you wish for.
fantasy.example: He had a fantasy about living on a beach.

meadows.definition: Large fields of grass, often with flowers.
meadows.example: The children ran through the green meadows.

picnics.definition: Meals eaten outside, usually on a blanket on the ground.
picnics.example: We love having picnics in the park.

reality.definition: The way things actually are, not how you imagine them.
reality.example: The reality of the job was harder than expected.

ongoing.example: The construction is an ongoing project.

battle.example: Getting the kids to bed is a daily battle.

persuading.definition: Trying to make someone agree to do something.
persuading.example: She spent an hour persuading him to come.

laughter.example: The room was filled with laughter.

audience.definition: A group of people watching or listening to a performance.
audience.example: The audience clapped after the show.

described.definition: Told about something using words so others can picture it.
described.example: She described the beautiful sunset to me.

boring.example: The meeting was long and boring.

dispiriting.definition: Making you feel hopeless or without energy.

frustrating.definition: Making you feel annoyed because you cannot do what you want.
frustrating.example: It is frustrating when the computer is slow.

taxing.definition: Using a lot of your energy; tiring and difficult.
taxing.example: Taking care of a baby is very taxing.

joyful.example: The wedding was a joyful occasion.

experience.definition: Something that happens to you or that you do, especially something important.

bogged.definition: Stuck and unable to move forward or make progress.
bogged.example: I got bogged down in paperwork.

minutiae.example: Don't worry about the minutiae; focus on the big picture.

nappies.definition: Soft cloth or paper worn by babies to catch waste (British English for diapers).
nappies.example: We need to buy more nappies for the baby.

tantrums.definition: Sudden bursts of anger, crying, or screaming, especially by a child.
tantrums.example: The toddler had a tantrum in the store.

toddler.definition: A very young child who is just learning to walk, usually age 1-3.
toddler.example: The toddler took her first steps today.

teenager.definition: A young person between 13 and 19 years old.

scraping.definition: Removing something by rubbing it with a hard tool.
scraping.example: She was scraping ice off the car window.

banana.definition: A long yellow fruit that is soft inside.
banana.example: I eat a banana every morning.

cracks.definition: Thin narrow gaps or openings in a surface.
cracks.example: Water leaked through the cracks in the wall.

headteacher.definition: The person in charge of a school (British English for principal).
headteacher.example: The headteacher called a meeting with parents.

summoning.definition: Officially asking or ordering someone to come to a place.
summoning.example: The boss was summoning everyone to a meeting.

perspective.definition: A way of thinking about something; the ability to see what is really important.
perspective.example: Try to see things from a different perspective.

core.definition: The most important or central part of something.
core.example: Trust is the core of any friendship.

soil.definition: The earth that plants grow in.
soil.example: Good soil helps plants grow strong.

nurtures.definition: Cares for and helps something or someone grow.
nurtures.example: A loving family nurtures a child's confidence.

inhibits.definition: Stops or slows down something from happening.
inhibits.example: Fear inhibits people from trying new things.

lean.definition: To rest against something for support.
lean.example: A child needs someone to lean on.

security.definition: The feeling of being safe and protected.
security.example: Children need a sense of security.

compromised.definition: Weakened or damaged so it does not work well.
compromised.example: His health was compromised by stress.

source.definition: The place where something comes from.
source.example: Family can be a source of strength.

strength.definition: The quality of being strong, either physically or emotionally.
strength.example: Her love gave him strength during hard times.

psychotherapist.definition: A person trained to help people with emotional or mental problems by talking with them.
psychotherapist.example: She visited a psychotherapist to help with her anxiety.

struggle.definition: To try very hard to do something difficult.
struggle.example: Many parents struggle with lack of sleep.

aspects.definition: Different parts or features of something.
aspects.example: There are many aspects of being a good parent.

opportunity.example: This job gave me the opportunity to travel.

dysfunctional.definition: Not working normally or in a healthy way.
dysfunctional.example: Growing up in a dysfunctional family can be hard.

objective.definition: A goal or purpose that you are trying to reach.
objective.example: The objective of this class is to improve your writing.

relevant.definition: Connected to what is being discussed or considered; important to the topic.
relevant.example: Only share information that is relevant to the discussion.

attune.definition: To make yourself aware of and sensitive to someone's feelings or needs.
attune.example: Good parents attune themselves to their child's emotions.

patterns.definition: Things that happen in the same way again and again.
patterns.example: She noticed patterns in her child's behavior.

conflict.definition: A serious disagreement or fight between people.
conflict.example: There was conflict between the two brothers.

withdrawal.definition: Pulling away from someone emotionally; becoming distant.
withdrawal.example: His withdrawal from the family worried everyone.

encourage.definition: To give someone support and confidence to do something.
encourage.example: Teachers should encourage students to ask questions.

babyhood.definition: The time when someone is a baby.
babyhood.example: She has no memories of her babyhood.

upbringing.definition: The way a child is raised and taught by their parents.
upbringing.example: Her strict upbringing shaped who she is today.

attitudes.definition: Ways of thinking or feeling about something.
attitudes.example: Positive attitudes help you face challenges.

pregnancy.definition: The time when a woman carries a baby inside her body before birth.
pregnancy.example: She exercised regularly during her pregnancy.

bond.definition: A strong feeling of closeness and connection between people.
bond.example: The bond between mother and child is very strong.

adolescent.definition: A young person who is developing from a child into an adult, usually a teenager.

satisfaction.definition: A good feeling you get when you achieve something or when things go well.
satisfaction.example: Finishing the project gave her great satisfaction.

manipulate.definition: To control or influence someone in a clever or unfair way.
manipulate.example: It is wrong to manipulate people to get what you want.

relate.definition: To connect with someone; to understand and communicate well with them.
relate.example: It is important to learn how to relate to others.

leisure.definition: Free time when you are not working.
leisure.example: She enjoys reading in her leisure time.

pursuits.definition: Activities that you spend time doing, especially for enjoyment.
pursuits.example: His leisure pursuits include hiking and painting.

vegetable.definition: A plant or part of a plant used as food, such as carrots or broccoli.
vegetable.example: Children should eat at least one vegetable at dinner.

coat.definition: A piece of warm clothing worn over other clothes.
coat.example: Put on your coat before going outside.

supports.definition: Holds up or gives help and encouragement to someone or something.
supports.example: A good friend supports you during hard times.

share.definition: To give part of something to others; to tell others about your thoughts or feelings.
share.example: I want to share what I have learned with you.

include.definition: To contain something as part of a whole.

feelings.definition: Emotions such as happiness, sadness, fear, or anger.
feelings.example: It is healthy to talk about your feelings.

approach.definition: A way of dealing with a situation or problem.
approach.example: We need a new approach to solve this problem.

particular.definition: Specific; one certain thing rather than others.
particular.example: Is there a particular reason you are late?

behaviour.definition: The way a person acts or conducts themselves.
behaviour.example: Good behaviour is rewarded at school.

interested.definition: Wanting to learn more about something or give it attention.
interested.example: She is very interested in science.

stand-up.definition: A type of comedy where one person tells jokes alone on stage.
stand-up.example: He performed a stand-up show last night.

dressed.definition: Wearing clothes; having clothes put on.
dressed.example: The children got dressed for school.

illnesses.definition: Diseases or periods of being sick.

childhood.definition: The time of life when you are a child.

unique.definition: Being the only one of its kind; special and different from everything else.

individual.definition: A single person, considered separately from others.
individual.example: Each individual has their own story.

plant.definition: A living thing that grows in the ground, with leaves and roots.
